name: Billy Preston
abstract: William Everett Preston (September 2, 1946 – June 6, 2006) was an American keyboardist, singer, and songwriter whose work encompassed R&B, rock, soul, funk, and gospel. Preston was a top session keyboardist in the 1960s, backing Little Richard, Sam Cooke, Ray Charles, the Everly Brothers, Reverend James Cleveland, the Beatles, and the Ro…

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: For the keyboards we had Billy Preston, we had Dr. John and we had Stevie Winwood... I was really glad to be a part of that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Scott
about_quotes.evidence: it seemed like the center of the pop music universe back then because there’d be like Billy Preston over here and the Carpenters over here... in another room.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Scott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Buckingham,
about_quotes.evidence: I saw all the great ones. When I first saw Aretha in ’69 it was the King Curtis band. ... Billy Preston on organ
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Buckingham, interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 25, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Billy Preston as the boy W.C. Handy... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, I mentioned Nelson before.
